skill.name: backend-migrations
skill.description: Guides database migration work with reversible changes, naming discipline, rollback planning, and zero-downtime deployment considerations.
skill.summary: Create focused, reversible database migrations with safer deployment practices.
skill.author: DevanB

security_audit.summary: Static command execution and weak cryptography findings are false positives from markdown text and normal product wording. One real low-risk issue remains: SKILL.md links to a parent-directory documentation path, which may cross the packaged skill boundary.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Misread as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Line 12 contains a markdown inline path, not Ruby or shell backtick execution. No executable command or user input interpolation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a bullet describing a directory path inside markdown backticks. There is no code block, command invocation, or execution context.

low_findings.title: Parent Directory Documentation Link
low_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE, low severity. Line 28 links to ../../../agent-os/standards/backend/migrations.md, which crosses out of the skill directory. This can break packaging and may encourage reading files outside the skill boundary, but no sensitive target or malicious intent is shown.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The parent-directory traversal is directly visible in the markdown link. The target appears to be documentation, so impact is limited without more evidence.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Lines 3 and 8 contain skill prose about migrations and Claude Code. No hash algorithm, encryption function, password storage, or cryptographic operation appears in the file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are descriptive text only. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, or any crypto API usage in SKILL.md.

dangerous_patterns.title: Relative Path Traversal in Documentation Reference
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill references documentation through a parent-directory path on line 28. This is not confirmed malicious, but marketplace packaging should prefer bundled files or direct safe references.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The ../../../ sequence is explicit and repeated by static analysis. The surrounding text frames it as a documentation link, not a data access or exfiltration path.

content.value_statement: Database migrations can create outages when changes are too broad or hard to roll back. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward focused, reversible migration plans.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation of focused database migration files.
Encourages reversible up and down migration behavior.
Prompts caution before editing migrations that may already be deployed.
Covers columns, indexes, constraints, foreign keys, and table renames.
Highlights zero-downtime planning for large production tables.
Separates schema changes from data migration concerns.
limitations: It does not include executable migration templates.
It depends on a referenced standards file outside the packaged skill.
It does not inspect a live database schema by itself.
It cannot guarantee deployment safety without project context.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Schema Change
use_cases.description: Create a narrow migration plan before adding tables, columns, indexes, or constraints.

use_cases.title: Review Rollback Safety
use_cases.description: Check whether a proposed migration has a practical rollback path before deployment.

use_cases.title: Prepare Production Database Changes
use_cases.description: Consider large-table indexes, compatibility, and data migration separation before release.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Migration
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new database change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the backend-migrations skill to plan a reversible migration for adding a nullable column to an existing table.

prompt_templates.title: Review an Existing Migration
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review before merging a migration.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the backend-migrations skill to review this migration for rollback safety, naming, and deployment risk.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Large Index Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the backend-migrations skill to design a safe plan for adding an index to a large production table.

prompt_templates.title: Separate Schema and Data Work
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the backend-migrations skill to split this schema change and data backfill into safer deployable steps.

output_examples.input: Add a column for tracking when users accepted terms.
output_examples.output: A focused migration plan with a nullable timestamp column, a clear rollback, and notes about application compatibility.

output_examples.input: Add an index to a large events table.
output_examples.output: A deployment-aware review that calls out lock risk, concurrent index options, and monitoring needs.

output_examples.input: Change a required column name in production.
output_examples.output: A staged approach using additive changes first, application updates second, and cleanup only after compatibility is confirmed.

best_practices: Keep each migration focused on one schema concern.
Write rollback behavior before treating the migration as complete.
Plan large-table changes with deployment and lock behavior in mind.
anti_patterns: Do not edit deployed migrations without confirming project policy.
Do not mix broad data backfills with unrelated schema changes.
Do not add production indexes without considering lock and runtime impact.

faq.question: Does this skill generate migration files?
faq.answer: It guides migration creation and review. Actual file output depends on the project framework and requested task.

faq.question: Can it handle rollback planning?
faq.answer: Yes. Reversibility and rollback methods are central parts of the guidance.

faq.question: Does it support zero-downtime deployments?
faq.answer: It highlights zero-downtime concerns, especially for large tables, indexes, and backwards-compatible schema changes.

faq.question: Is it tied to one database engine?
faq.answer: The visible skill text is general. Database-specific behavior needs project context.

faq.question: Can it modify existing deployed migrations?
faq.answer: It warns that existing migrations require caution. Project policy should decide whether edits are allowed.

faq.question: Is there any security concern?
faq.answer: The main concern is a parent-directory documentation link. It should be bundled or rewritten before publication.
